authors: Kapural L, Yu C, Doust MW, Gliner BE, Vallejo R, Sitzman BT, Amirdelfan K, Morgan DM, Yearwood TL, Bundschu R, Yang T, Benyamin R, Burgher AH
title: Comparison of 10-kHz High-Frequency and Traditional Low-Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation for Chronic Back and Leg Pain: 24-Month Results
study_question: Is 10-kHz high-frequency spinal cord stimulation superior to traditional low-frequency SCS for chronic back and leg pain?
population_assessed: 198 subjects with chronic intractable back and leg pain refractory to conservative therapy, mean age 54.9 years.
outcome_measures: Responder rate (≥50% pain reduction), pain remission, VAS scores, ODI, patient satisfaction
inclusion_criteria: Chronic intractable trunk/limb pain refractory to conservative therapy ≥3 months, back and leg pain VAS ≥5.0, ODI 41-80.
answer_to_study_question: HF10 therapy demonstrated superiority over traditional SCS at 24 months for back pain (76.5% vs 49.3% responders) and leg pain (72.9% vs 49.3%).
